mnemonics: RBT: Really Big Tables (for RDBMS) - Recall that RBT (Relational Based Topics) are the advanced SQL features that help you manage Really Big Tables efficiently.
keyPoints: RBT stands for Relational Based Topics, which covers advanced SQL features beyond basic CRUD operations.
Key features include complex Joins, Subqueries, Set Operations (UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT), and advanced functions for data aggregation and manipulation.
These features are essential for performing sophisticated data retrieval, analysis, and ensuring data integrity across complex database schemas.
commonMistakes: Confusing the usage of IN, EXISTS, and JOIN in subqueries, leading to inefficient or incorrect queries.
Forgetting to handle NULL values properly in set operations and aggregate functions, which can skew results.
